# Copilot instructions for this repo

## Build, test, and lint
| Area | Command | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Frontend build | `cd nextjs-frontend && pnpm run build` | Next.js production build. |
| Frontend tests | `cd nextjs-frontend && pnpm run test` | Jest. |
| Frontend single test | `cd nextjs-frontend && pnpm run test -- <test-file-or-pattern>` | Pass a file path or Jest pattern. |
| Frontend lint | `cd nextjs-frontend && pnpm run lint` | ESLint (see `eslint.config.mjs`). |
| Frontend typecheck | `cd nextjs-frontend && pnpm run tsc` | TS compiler check. |
| Backend tests | `cd fastapi_backend && uv run pytest` | Pytest. |
| Backend single test | `cd fastapi_backend && uv run pytest tests/test_file.py::test_name` | Standard pytest node selection. |
| Backend lint | `cd fastapi_backend && uv run ruff check .` | Ruff is configured via pre-commit. |
| Backend format | `cd fastapi_backend && uv run ruff format .` | Ruff formatter. |
| Full lint suite | `pre-commit run --all-files` | Runs ruff/ruff-format, frontend lint/prettier/tsc, and OpenAPI/client generation. |

## High-level architecture
- Monorepo with **FastAPI backend** in `fastapi_backend/` and **Next.js App Router frontend** in `nextjs-frontend/`.
- Backend entrypoint is `fastapi_backend/app/main.py`; routes live under `fastapi_backend/app/routes`, models in `app/models.py`, schemas in `app/schemas.py`, auth in `app/users.py`, and settings in `app/config.py`.
- Async SQLAlchemy is used for Postgres (`app/database.py`), with `fastapi-users` providing JWT auth routes and user management.
- OpenAPI schema is generated by `fastapi_backend/commands/generate_openapi_schema.py` and written to `OPENAPI_OUTPUT_FILE` from `.env`. `fastapi_backend/watcher.py` re-runs mypy and schema generation on backend changes.
- Frontend API client is generated by `@hey-api/openapi-ts` (`nextjs-frontend/openapi-ts.config.ts`) into `nextjs-frontend/app/openapi-client` and re-exported via `app/clientService.ts`. `nextjs-frontend/watcher.js` re-generates the client when the OpenAPI file changes.

## Key conventions
- **OpenAPI-driven client:** Treat `nextjs-frontend/app/openapi-client` and `openapi.json` as generated artifacts; update backend routes/schemas and regenerate instead of editing these directly.
- **Operation IDs depend on route tags:** `simple_generate_unique_route_id` uses the first route tag plus the route name, and `generate_openapi_schema.py` strips the tag prefix. Keep route tags stable to avoid noisy client method renames.
- **Password policy is duplicated:** Backend validation in `fastapi_backend/app/users.py` and frontend Zod validation in `nextjs-frontend/lib/definitions.ts` must stay in sync when changing rules.
- **Client config is side-effectful:** `app/clientService.ts` imports `lib/clientConfig.ts` for base URL setup; keep that import when reorganizing API code.
